my_thoughts: The denial works: no self-generating force, external methods necessary (must be shaken). The student asked self-generating question (seeking self-generating solution), which shows they understand: realization = force (magical thinking). They'll likely accept the denial. The episode is complete: method failure → normalization → self-generating → denial. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Two-turn pattern: T1: Student reported method failure (frustrated empirical failure)—normalization: method entropy = normal, must change methods. T2: Student asked self-generating question ('Does the realization of being asleep create its own force for awakening?')—seeking self-generating solution via asks for functional mechanism and seeks shortcuts. They understand: realization = force (magical thinking). The comparison_contrast mechanism denies: no self-generating force, external methods necessary ('If one realizes one is asleep, one must study means and methods to awake... This is why one cannot awake by oneself, why elaborate methods are necessary—one must be shaken and shaken'). Pattern complete: method failure → normalization → self-generating question → denial. They've shifted: realization ≠ force, external methods = necessary.
visible_response:
If one realizes one is asleep, one must study means and methods to awake, but it
must cease to be a word;
it must become a fact based on observation. Only then is it
possible to speak with more precision and more practically about it.
When one realizes
one is asleep, at that moment one is already half-awake, but it is not long enough;
the
next moment something begins to turn in one's head and one gets carried away into
sleep again.
This is why one cannot awake by oneself, why elaborate methods are
necessary—one must be shaken and shaken.
